text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-hook-form repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: a subscription created via `form.subscribe({ formState: { ... }, callback })` receives a payload that includes the `name` of the field involved in the event that triggered it. If you call `clearErrors('firstName')` and then, later, trigger an unrelated form-state update that has no field name of its own (for example submitting the form via `handleSubmit`), the subscriber's callback fires with `name` still set to `'firstName'` — a stale value left over from the earlier `clearErrors` call — instead of `undefined`. In other words, the per-event metadata (`name`/`type`) from one notification is being persisted into form state and bleeding into later, unrelated notifications. Fix the library source so that per-event metadata is not persisted across updates and each subscriber notification reports only the name (if any) of the event that actually triggered it. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (via `npm install -g pnpm@10.34.5 && CYPRESS_INSTALL_BINARY=0 pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts`). Run tests with `node_modules/.bin/jest --config ./scripts/jest/jest.config.js src/__tests__/useForm/subscribe.test.tsx`.

text: I’ll trace how `subscribe` builds notification payloads and how `clearErrors`/submit updates flow through that state, then patch the source and run the targeted Jest test (plus any focused checks needed).

The relevant state object currently carries optional `name` and `type` fields alongside persistent form state. I’m narrowing the inspection to every `_subjects.state.next` call and the subscriber adapter, because the fix must clear metadata at the notification boundary without breaking field-scoped filtering.

text: The leak is confirmed: `shouldRenderFormState` feeds event payloads into `_setFormState`, which merges all keys into `_formState`; when a later payload omits `name`, the old value remains available through the callback’s persistent-state spread. I’ll make `_setFormState` persist only actual form-state keys, while leaving `name`/`type` on the current event payload, and add a regression covering `clearErrors` followed by submit.

The source change now strips `name` and `type` before updating persistent form state, so each notification still exposes its own metadata through `formState` but cannot leave it behind. The regression test uses the exact sequence from the report and verifies the later submit notification has an undefined name. I’m running the requested Jest file now.

text: The targeted suite passes: 14 tests, including the new regression. I’m doing a final diff and type/lint-oriented check to ensure the metadata omission is cleanly typed and the test does not rely on an accidental callback absence.

Fixed the stale subscription metadata bug in [createFormControl.ts](/home/user/workspace/src/logic/createFormControl.ts:1857) by excluding per-event `name` and `type` from persistent form state.

Added a regression test covering `clearErrors('firstName')` followed by submit.
